response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering experience and infrastructure skills, the role requires specialized embedded security firmware expertise that is fundamentally misaligned with their career trajectory. The resume shows primarily web/backend development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than embedded C development, security technologies (encryption, authentication, tamper detection), or networking protocols. Most critically, the role explicitly requires native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is entirely absent from the resume. The candidate lacks the domain-specific knowledge in embedded Linux kernel work, networking (Layer-2/3, switch operation, NETCONF, SNMP), and security architecture that this position demands.
strengths: Extensive software development background spanning 18+ years across multiple languages and platforms demonstrates adaptability and deep technical foundation
Proven expertise in infrastructure, CI/CD, Docker, and development environments shows systems-level thinking transferable to embedded contexts
Track record of learning new technologies and teaching complex concepts indicates ability to acquire new domain knowledge
concerns: No native-level Japanese language proficiency; role requires this as a hard requirement, making this a complete blocker

Career focused on web/backend development (TypeScript, Node.js) rather than embedded firmware; minimal C programming experience (brief stint at National Instruments) despite role requiring strong C proficiency

No demonstrated expertise in security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, authentication systems) or networking protocols (Layer-2/3, NETCONF, SNMP, Telemetry); embedded security is outside candidate's domain

key_factors.factor: C programming and embedded Linux expertise
